content: A water resources project with an expected life of 25 years has to be designed for an acceptable risk of $5 \%$ against a design flood. The return period for the design flood (in years) is __________ (rounded off to the nearest integer).
explanation:
To determine the return period for the design flood, follow these steps:

Given Values:

Expected life of the project, $ n = 25 \text{ years} $

Acceptable risk, $\text{Risk} = 5\% $

Return period, $ T = ? \text{ (in years)} $

Formula for Risk:

The risk for the event is given by:

$ \text{Risk} = 1 - \left(1 - \frac{1}{T}\right)^n $

Substituting the given values into the risk formula:

$ 0.05 = 1 - \left(1 - \frac{1}{T}\right)^{25} $

Solving for $ T $:

Rearrange the equation to find $ T $:

$ \left(1 - \frac{1}{T}\right)^{25} = 0.95 $

Taking the 25th root on both sides and solving for $ 1 - \frac{1}{T} $, you can use logarithms or approximation methods. Solving would yield:

$ T \approx 488 \text{ years} $

Therefore, the return period $ T $ for the design flood, rounded to the nearest integer, is approximately 488 years.

content: A flood control structure having an expected life of n  years is designed by considering a flood of return period T years. When T = n, and n $$\to$$ $$\infty$$, the structures hydrologic risk of failure in percentage is ____________. (rounded off to one decimal place)
explanation:
R = 1 $$-$$ qn

$$\Rightarrow$$ R = 1 $$-$$ (1 $$-$$ P)n

R = 1 $$-$$ $${\left[ {1 - {1 \over T}} \right]^n}$$

Given than T = n and n $$\to$$ $$\infty$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ R = 1 $$-$$ $$\left[ {\mathop {\lim }\limits_{n \to \infty } {{\left( {1 - {1 \over n}} \right)}^n}} \right]$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ R = 1 $$-$$ $$\left[ {\mathop {\lim }\limits_{n \to \infty } {{\left\{ {1 + \left( { - {1 \over n}} \right)} \right\}}^n}} \right]$$ ....... (i)

We know that,

$$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{n \to \infty } {(1 + x)^{1/x}} = e$$,

So let's take $${{ - 1} \over n} = y$$

$$\therefore$$ $$n = {{ - 1} \over y}$$

at n = $$\infty$$; y = 0

Now, $$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{n \to \infty } {\left( {1 - {1 \over n}} \right)^n}$$

$$ = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{y \to 0} {(1 + y)^{ - 1/y}}$$

$$ = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{y \to 0} {(1 + y)^{{1 \over y}( - 1)}}$$

$$ = {\left[ {\mathop {\lim }\limits_{y \to 0} {{(1 + y)}^{{1 \over y}}}} \right]^{ - 1}}$$

$$ = {e^{ - 1}}$$

Put in eq. (i)

$$\therefore$$ $$R = 1 - {e^{ - 1}}$$

$$R = 1 - {1 \over e}$$

$$R = 0.6321$$ i.e. 63.2%
